title: 85-lesson milestone
description: 15 lessons from the finish — checkpoint at 85 WPM
text: Eighty-five lessons. Fifteen to the finish. Your speed has nearly doubled since lesson one, and your accuracy is steady where it counts. The final stretch is not about adding new skills — it is about hardening what you have so that high speed feels effortless, even on a Monday morning after a long weekend.

tips.text: Eighty-five down, fifteen to go. This is the part of the run where you stop adding and start hardening.
Eighty-five words a minute is working speed for a writer or a programmer. You are no longer learning to type; you are typing.

tips.target: Eighty-five lessons. Fifteen to the finish. Your speed has nearly doubled since lesson one, and your accuracy is steady where it counts. The final stretch is not

tips.text: The lessons that remain do one job: turn earned speed into automatic speed, the kind that holds up on a tired Monday.

tips.target: about adding new skills — it is about hardening what you have so that high speed feels effortless, even on a Monday morning after a long weekend.

character_tips.anna: Eighty-five, with fifteen to go. The job now is to make this speed feel like nothing at all.
character_tips.maxim: This is professional speed. The work left is repetition, not revelation. Keep showing up.
character_tips.knopych: The finish line is in view. Hold your rhythm and let it carry you in.
character_tips.klavochka: You have nearly doubled what you started with. Steady the last stretch and it is yours. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
